title: New Medical Breakthroughs Offer Hope for Cancer Patients
subtitle: Recent clinical trials reveal new ways to treat cancer while reducing the need for harsh chemotherapy.

summary.en: Medical researchers have recently shared exciting findings at a major international cancer conference. A new DNA test for breast cancer patients could help millions avoid unnecessary chemotherapy. Meanwhile, a new 'smart' injection has shown the ability to shrink or even remove tumors in patients with head, neck, and lung cancers. Another study suggests that combining new drugs with existing treatments may improve survival rates for lung cancer patients. These developments represent a significant shift in how doctors approach cancer care. Experts believe these findings could change medical practice and improve the quality of life for many people.
summary.zh: 醫學研究人員近期在一場大型國際癌症會議上分享了令人振奮的發現。一項針對乳癌患者的新型 DNA 檢測，有望幫助數百萬人免除不必要的化學治療。同時，一種新的「智慧型」注射劑已展現出能縮小甚至消除頭頸癌及肺癌患者腫瘤的能力。另一項研究則建議，將新藥與現有療法結合，或許能提升肺癌患者的存活率。這些進展代表醫師處理癌症照護的方式出現了重大轉變。專家相信，這些發現可能改變醫療實務，並改善許多人的生活品質。

paragraphs.en: Medical experts are sharing positive news about cancer treatment after presenting several new study results at a major international conference in Chicago. These findings suggest that doctors may soon be able to offer more effective care while sparing patients from the difficult side effects of traditional treatments like chemotherapy. Chemotherapy is a common medical treatment that uses strong drugs to kill cancer cells, but it often causes patients to feel very tired, lose their hair, and suffer from nausea.
paragraphs.zh: 醫學專家在芝加哥舉行的一場大型國際會議上發表了多項研究成果，為癌症治療帶來了正面消息。這些發現顯示，醫師未來或許能提供更有效的照護，同時讓病患免受化學治療等傳統療法所帶來的嚴重副作用。化學治療是一種常見的醫療手段，利用強效藥物殺死癌細胞，但往往會導致病患感到極度疲憊、掉髮以及噁心嘔吐。

paragraphs.en: According to BBC Health, a new DNA test could allow millions of breast cancer patients to safely avoid chemotherapy. Researchers led by University College London studied over 4,000 patients across several countries. They used a test called Prosigna to look at 50 specific genes that affect how breast cancer grows. The results showed that about two-thirds of the patients in the study had a low risk of the cancer returning. These patients were able to skip chemotherapy and use hormone therapy instead. Experts believe this will help thousands of people avoid the physical and emotional pain of unnecessary treatment.
paragraphs.zh: 根據英國廣播公司健康頻道（BBC Health）報導，一項新的去氧核糖核酸（DNA）檢測可能讓數百萬名乳癌患者安全地免除化學治療。由倫敦大學學院（University College London）領導的研究團隊針對多國超過四千名病患進行研究。他們使用一種名為 Prosigna 的檢測，觀察五十種影響乳癌生長的特定基因。結果顯示，研究中約三分之二的病患癌症復發風險較低。這些病患可以跳過化學治療，改用荷爾蒙療法。專家相信，這將有助於數千人免於承受不必要治療所帶來的身心痛苦。

paragraphs.en: In another breakthrough, a new type of medicine is showing great promise for patients with head, neck, and lung cancers. The Guardian Health reports that a 'smart' injection called amivantamab can attack cancer in three different ways. It blocks proteins that help tumors grow and also helps the body’s immune system fight the disease. In a trial involving 11 countries, the jab caused tumors to shrink in many patients. For 15 people in the study, the drug completely removed their tumors. One patient, Carl Walsh, explained that the treatment was much easier to receive than his previous care. Because it is given as a simple injection under the skin, it is more convenient for patients and does not require the long hours of an intravenous drip.
paragraphs.zh: 在另一項突破中，一種新型藥物對頭頸癌及肺癌患者展現了極大的潛力。《衛報健康》（The Guardian Health）報導指出，一種稱為 amivantamab 的「智慧型」注射劑能以三種不同方式攻擊癌細胞。它能阻斷有助於腫瘤生長的蛋白質，並協助人體的免疫系統對抗疾病。在一項涵蓋十一國的試驗中，該注射劑使許多病患的腫瘤縮小。研究中有十五人的腫瘤完全消失。其中一名病患華許（Carl Walsh）表示，這種治療比他過去接受的照護輕鬆許多。由於只需透過皮下注射，對病患而言更為便利，且無需耗費長時間進行靜脈點滴注射。

paragraphs.en: While these new treatments offer hope, doctors are also looking at how to improve existing therapies. MedPage Today reports on a study for lung cancer patients that combined a new drug with a common immunotherapy treatment. Immunotherapy is a type of treatment that helps the patient's own immune system recognize and destroy cancer cells. The study found that adding the new drug, called sac-TMT, significantly lowered the risk of the disease getting worse compared to using immunotherapy alone. While the combination treatment did cause more side effects, researchers believe the early results show a clear trend toward better survival rates.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管這些新療法帶來了希望，醫師們也正在研究如何改善現有的治療方式。《今日醫學頁面》（MedPage Today）報導了一項針對肺癌患者的研究，該研究將一種新藥與常見的免疫療法相結合。免疫療法是一種協助病患自身免疫系統辨識並摧毀癌細胞的治療方式。研究發現，與單獨使用免疫療法相比，加入名為 sac-TMT 的新藥能顯著降低病情惡化的風險。雖然這種合併治療確實造成了較多副作用，但研究人員認為，初步結果顯示出存活率提升的明確趨勢。

paragraphs.en: Despite the excitement, experts remain cautious about how these new methods will be used in the future. Some doctors noted that while new drug combinations can be powerful, they also bring more toxicity, or harmful effects, to the body. Natalie Vokes, a doctor at the MD Anderson Cancer Center, pointed out that it is important to balance the benefits of a new drug with the quality of life for the patient. She emphasized that while the early results are impressive, more global studies are needed to confirm these findings for all types of patients.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管各界感到振奮，專家對於這些新方法未來如何應用仍持謹慎態度。部分醫師指出，雖然新的藥物組合效果強大，但也可能對身體造成更大的毒性或有害影響。德州大學安德森癌症中心（MD Anderson Cancer Center）的醫師沃克斯（Natalie Vokes）強調，在藥物療效與病患生活品質之間取得平衡至關重要。她強調，雖然初步結果令人印象深刻，但仍需進行更多全球性研究，以確認這些發現是否適用於所有類型的病患。

paragraphs.en: These developments are being described by many in the medical field as 'practice-changing.' For patients like Karen Bonham, who participated in the breast cancer study, the news is a huge relief. She explained that a cancer diagnosis often makes life feel uncertain, and knowing that she could avoid chemotherapy was a life-changing moment. As researchers continue to analyze these trials, the goal remains to provide the best possible care while reducing the burden on patients. With more research, doctors hope to define exactly which patients will benefit most from these new, targeted approaches, ensuring that everyone receives the treatment that is right for them.
paragraphs.zh: 醫學界許多人士將這些進展形容為「改變臨床實務」。對於像邦漢（Karen Bonham）這樣參與乳癌研究的病患來說，這項消息是一大解脫。她解釋說，確診癌症往往讓人感到生活充滿不確定性，而得知自己可以免除化學治療，是改變人生的時刻。隨著研究人員持續分析這些試驗，目標依然是在減輕病患負擔的同時，提供最佳的照護。透過更多研究，醫師希望能精確定義哪些病患最能從這些新的標靶療法中獲益，確保每個人都能獲得最適合自己的治療。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To prevent someone from experiencing something unpleasant.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 使免受（不愉快的經歷或痛苦）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作形容詞（節約的），這裡作動詞用。文中：These findings suggest that doctors may soon be able to offer more effective care while sparing patients from the difficult side effects of traditional treatments like chemotherapy.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To stop something from moving or functioning normally.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（街區、積木），這裡作動詞用。文中：It blocks proteins that help tumors grow and also helps the body’s immune system fight the disease.

keyTerms.definitionEn: An injection of a medicine or vaccine.
keyTerms.noteZh: 口語用法，容易與動詞「戳、刺」混淆。文中：In a trial involving 11 countries, the jab caused tumors to shrink in many patients.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A heavy load or something that causes worry or difficulty.
keyTerms.noteZh: 在醫學語境中指對患者造成的心理或生理壓力。文中：As researchers continue to analyze these trials, the goal remains to provide the best possible care while reducing the burden on patients.

sources.name: BBC Health
sources.title: Millions of breast cancer patients could safely avoid chemotherapy, study suggests

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Cancer jab can eradicate entire tumours in patients, trial shows

sources.name: MedPage Today
sources.title: Drug Combo for Lung Cancer Beats Pembrolizumab in First-Line Trial

quiz.question: According to the article, what is the primary advantage of the 'smart' injection amivantamab compared to traditional intravenous treatments?

options.A: It is significantly cheaper than standard chemotherapy drugs.
options.B: It is more convenient because it does not require long hours of an intravenous drip.
options.C: It is guaranteed to completely remove tumors in every single patient.
options.D: It has no side effects compared to the toxicity of other cancer treatments.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第三段明確指出，amivantamab 作為皮下注射，比傳統的靜脈點滴更方便，且不需要長時間的點滴注射。選項 A 文章未提及價格比較；選項 C 錯誤，文中提到 15 人完全消除腫瘤，並非所有患者；選項 D 錯誤，文中並未說它完全沒有副作用，僅提到它更方便。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the medical community's attitude toward the new cancer treatments described in the article?

options.A: They believe these treatments are perfect and should replace all existing therapies immediately.
options.B: They are concerned that these treatments are too expensive for the average patient to afford.
options.C: They are optimistic about the results but emphasize the need for caution and further global verification.
options.D: They think that chemotherapy is still the most effective treatment for all types of cancer.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第五段，儘管研究結果令人興奮，專家們仍保持謹慎，並強調需要更多全球性的研究來確認這些發現，並考慮藥物帶來的毒性對生活品質的影響。選項 A 過於絕對；選項 B 文中未提及費用問題；選項 D 與文章強調尋找替代化療方案的宗旨相違背。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'toxicity' most likely mean?

options.A: The speed at which a new medicine works.
options.B: The harmful or poisonous effects that a substance can have.
options.C: The cost of developing a new medical treatment.
options.D: The level of popularity a drug has among doctors.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在醫學語境中，toxicity 指的是藥物對身體產生的毒性或有害副作用。文章提到新藥組合雖然強大，但也會帶來更多的 toxicity，這與前文提到的副作用（side effects）概念呼應。其他選項皆不符合醫學領域對此詞彙的定義。

quiz.question: What is the central message of this article?

options.A: Chemotherapy is the only proven method for curing breast, lung, and neck cancers.
options.B: New medical breakthroughs are offering more effective and patient-friendly cancer treatments, though further research is required.
options.C: The Prosigna DNA test is the most important medical discovery mentioned in the recent Chicago conference.
options.D: Medical experts are shifting their focus entirely away from treating cancer to only focusing on patient comfort.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章總結了多項癌症治療的新進展（如基因檢測、智慧注射、組合療法），並強調這些進展旨在提高治療效果並減輕患者負擔，同時也提到專家對於未來應用的謹慎態度。選項 A 與文意相反；選項 C 僅是文章的一部分；選項 D 錯誤，文章重點仍在於「治療效果」與「生活品質」的平衡。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
